concepts.finding: No single dataset covers full search intelligence pipeline. Must compose: MS MARCO (retrieval), AOL/ORCAS (clicks), TREC (relevance), ClueWeb (content).
concepts.implication: Intent clustering needs assembled pipeline combining ORCAS (click signals) with MS MARCO and web crawl.
concepts.open_question: What is minimal dataset composition for reliable intent discovery?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: ORCAS + MS MARCO produces more coherent clusters than either alone.

concepts.finding: MS MARCO has ~1M queries but weak labels from Bing click logs, not human judgments.
concepts.implication: Click-derived labels biased by SERP position effects. Intent signals inherit this bias.
concepts.open_question: How much bias do click-derived labels introduce into downstream intent models?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: MS MARCO clusters show systematic bias toward popular high-rank pages.

concepts.finding: Click datasets essential for intent inference but suffer position bias, trust bias, privacy filtering, missing negatives.
concepts.implication: Raw CTR is misleading intent proxy. Position bias correction (IPW) required.
concepts.open_question: What debiasing method is most effective for intent inference from click logs?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: DBN/UBM click models produce intent labels with higher external validity than raw aggregation.

concepts.finding: No large multi-class intent dataset exists. Intent is binary or weakly inferred. No standardized taxonomy.
concepts.implication: Multi-class intent (informational, transactional, navigational, commercial) needs better datasets.
concepts.open_question: Can multi-class intent labels be inferred without manual annotation?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: 5+ intent categories can be induced from query-click patterns via unsupervised clustering.

concepts.finding: No open dataset includes full SERP layout. Commercial APIs not reproducible.
concepts.implication: SERP features contain rich intent signals inaccessible in current datasets.
concepts.open_question: How much does SERP context improve intent classification vs query-only?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: SERP snippet text input improves intent F1 by >5% over query-only models.

concepts.finding: ORCAS: 10M query-click mappings lacks session context. AOL: session structure but outdated (2006).
concepts.implication: Session context (sequences, reformulations) critical. Single-query models miss sequential behavior.
concepts.open_question: How many prior session queries provide useful context?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: 3-5 prior queries in session-aware models outperform single-query by >10% F1.

concepts.finding: Critical gaps: content gap detection, cannibalization detection, query-to-page mapping must be inferred.
concepts.implication: Current datasets don't support direct learning of these tasks. Heuristic inference required.
concepts.open_question: Can content gaps be detected from query clustering without page content?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Low page-coverage-diversity intent groups reliably indicate content gaps.

concepts.finding: Embedding generation (BERT, E5, OpenAI) required preprocessing for clustering and retrieval.
concepts.implication: Embedding quality is foundation of downstream clustering. Choice significantly impacts coherence.
concepts.open_question: Which embedding model produces most intent-aware representations for short queries?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: E5 retrieval embeddings produce more intent-coherent clusters than general MiniLM.

concepts.finding: Temporal dynamics ignored: query trends, seasonality, ranking changes not captured.
concepts.implication: Static snapshot models miss temporal evolution. Time-aware embeddings and updating needed.
concepts.open_question: How much does temporal context improve intent prediction?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Time-aware embeddings (week/season features) improve classification for time-sensitive queries.

concepts.finding: Starter stack: MS MARCO, BEIR, ORCAS, AOL logs, Common Crawl, Quora Pairs.
concepts.implication: Pipeline: embeddings on MS MARCO, BM25+dense retrieval, ORCAS weak supervision, clustering, gap detection.
concepts.open_question: What is optimal production intent discovery pipeline?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: 6-step pipeline identifies actionable content gaps with >70% precision.

concepts.finding: No unified dataset combining queries, SERPs, clicks, content exists. Forces heavy preprocessing.
concepts.implication: Deepest need is unified multimodal dataset. Until then, assemble from heterogeneous sources.
concepts.open_question: What entity could create a unified search intelligence dataset?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Unified dataset would accelerate intent modeling 2-3x vs fragmented approach.
